criteria: Real JSON Schema within Structured Outputs' supported subset — no recursive schemas, no numerical min/max constraints, no complex regex — rejected (never silently emitted) if it relies on any of those.

entries.label: good
entries.rationale: Flat, non-recursive object schema. No numerical min/max constraints (limit has no bounds — bounding happens in application code, not the schema). No regex patterns. Uses a plain enum for state instead of a pattern. Fully within Structured Outputs' supported subset.

entries.label: good
entries.rationale: Nested (an array of objects) but not recursive — the item schema never references itself or an ancestor. No min/max, no regex. This is the derivation shape AutoGen/Semantic Kernel produce from typed function signatures, within the supported subset.

entries.label: bad
entries.rationale: `children` items reference the schema's own root (`$ref: "#"`) — a recursive schema, explicitly unsupported by Structured Outputs' constrained-decoding compiler. This must be rejected outright, not silently emitted as if it would work.

entries.label: bad
entries.rationale: `minimum`/`maximum` on `level` are numerical bound constraints, explicitly unsupported. `pattern` on `ticket_id` is a complex regex, also explicitly unsupported. Both must be caught and rejected — bound-checking and format-validation belong in application code after generation, not encoded in the schema itself.
